prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ (most likely site for nucleophilic attack)
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) — no Gaussian basis, no functional
Geometry provenance: user-supplied ferrocene.xyz (21 atoms); single-point charges on this fixed geometry (no optimisation)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0,1) for the N reference; cation doublet (+1,2) and anion doublet (−1,2) for N±1 states
Software/backend: xtb backend via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (program="xtb")
Convergence & caveats: all three xtb single-points converged; integrity.status="ok", trustworthy=true.  Warnings returned by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.  Density-fitting does not apply to xTB; the result is an approximate conceptual-DFT index suitable for qualitative site ranking, not an absolute reactivity rate.

Result
• Most electrophilic atom (susceptible to nucleophilic attack): Fe (atom 11) with Fukui f⁺ = 0.22885456.
• Most nucleophilic atom (susceptible to electrophilic attack): Fe (atom 11) with Fukui f⁻ = 0.25938191.
